verses.1: For the music director. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Hear this, all peoples; Listen, all inhabitants of the world,
verses.2: Both low and high, Rich and poor together.
verses.3: My mouth will speak wisdom, And the meditation of my heart will be understanding.
verses.4: I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will express my riddle on the harp.
verses.5: Why should I fear in days of adversity, When the injustice of those who betray me surrounds me,
verses.6: Those who trust in their wealth And boast in the abundance of their riches?
verses.7: No one can by any means redeem another Or give God a ransom for him—
verses.8: For the redemption of his soul is priceless, And he should cease imagining forever—
verses.9: That he might live on eternally, That he might not undergo decay.
verses.10: For he sees that even wise people die; The foolish and the stupid alike perish And leave their wealth to others.
verses.11: Their inner thought is that their houses are forever And their dwelling places to all generations; They have named their lands after their own names.
verses.12: But man in his splendor will not endure; He is like the animals that perish.
verses.13: This is the way of those who are foolish, And of those after them who approve their words. Selah
verses.14: Like sheep they sink down to Sheol; Death will be their shepherd; And the upright will rule over them in the morning, And their form shall be for Sheol to consume So that they have no lofty home.
verses.15: But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol, For He will receive me. Selah
verses.16: Do not be afraid when a person becomes rich, When the splendor of his house is increased;
verses.17: For when he dies, he will take nothing with him; His wealth will not descend after him.
verses.18: Though while he lives he congratulates himself— And though people praise you when you do well for yourself—
verses.19: He will go to the generation of his fathers; They will never see the light.
verses.20: Mankind in its splendor, yet without understanding, Is like the animals that perish.
